title: INDEPENDENT LIVING PROGRAM - 211info
text: IMPACT NW BRENTWOOD-DARLINGTON COMMUNITY CENTER INDEPENDENT LIVING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps foster youth gain the skills they need to successfully transition out of foster care and into adulthood. Participants set and achieve attainable goals during one-on-one meetings, weekly job readiness classes, and social and skill-building activities and events. 7211 SE 62nd Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Foster youth ages 16-21 Hours: Varies by service Email (503) 867-2188 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 867-2188 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Multnomah County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Services: Life Skills Education * Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY FACILITIES/CENTERS - BRENTWOOD-DARLINGTON COMMUNITY CENTER EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - SUN YOUTH ADVOCACY PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - HEALTHY FAMILIES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - PARENT CHILD DEVELOPMENT SERVICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - PARENT CHILD THERAPEUTIC SERVICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SAFE AND TOGETHER INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SUN COMMUNITY SCHOOLS SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - EARLY CHILDHOOD FATHERING PROJECT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
